theory.intro: Elapsed time is how much time passes between a start time and a finish time. Count on from the start, jumping to easy 'landmark' times.
facts: There are $60$ minutes in $1$ hour, so times 'roll over' at $60$, not $100$.
**Count on** in steps: first up to the next o'clock, then whole hours, then the leftover minutes.
In $24$-hour time, afternoon hours are $12$ to $23$; e.g. $2{:}30$ pm $= 14{:}30$.
steps: Write the start time and the finish time.
Count on to the next full hour, then add whole hours, then the remaining minutes.
Add the jumps together to get the elapsed time.
